ogTitle: Point Samson vs Popanyinning: Climate Safety

heading: Point Samson vs Popanyinning — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Point Samson, Western Australia and Popanyinning, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Point Samson has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Popanyinning (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Point Samson or Popanyinning safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Point Samson has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Popanyinning scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Point Samson scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Popanyinning scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Point Samson or Popanyinning?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Point Samson has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
